mnemonics: DEADLOCK Prevention Acronym: SHoRT (Spoilation, Hold and Wait, Rollback, Timeout)
DEADLOCK Avoidance Acronym: SABOTAGE (Safe state, Avoidance, Banker's algorithm, Ordering, Tracking, Allocation, Granting, Execution)
keyPoints: Deadlock Prevention: Proactively design system to eliminate one of the four necessary conditions (Mutual Exclusion, Hold and Wait, No Preemption, Circular Wait)
Deadlock Avoidance: Use algorithms like Banker's Algorithm to ensure system never enters unsafe state where deadlock might occur
Deadlock Detection & Recovery: Allow deadlocks to occur, then detect them (using resource allocation graphs) and recover through process termination or resource preemption
commonMistakes: Confusing deadlock prevention with avoidance - prevention eliminates conditions, avoidance dynamically avoids unsafe states
Assuming the Banker's Algorithm is used for prevention when it's actually for avoidance
Thinking that ignoring deadlocks (Ostrich algorithm) is a viable solution for all systems when it's only practical for some environments
